skill.name: pdf
skill.description: Comprehensive PDF manipulation toolkit for extracting text and tables, creating new PDFs, merging/splitting documents, and handling forms. When Claude needs to fill in a PDF form or programmatically process, generate, or analyze PDF documents at scale.
skill.summary: Comprehensive PDF manipulation toolkit for extracting text and tables, creating new PDFs, merging/splitting documents, and handling forms.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 217 potential issues, but most are false positives from markdown documentation files. External command detections are code examples in backticks (markdown formatting), not actual shell execution. Filesystem operations in Python scripts are legitimate PDF/JSON processing with user-provided paths. No confirmed malicious patterns detected.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Patterns
medium_findings.description: Static analysis detected shell command patterns in documentation files. These are markdown code examples demonstrating command-line tool usage (qpdf, pdftotext, pdfimages), not actual executable code. All commands are intended for user reference only.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown documentation files contain backtick-quoted command examples for educational purposes, not executable shell code

low_findings.title: Filesystem Write Operations
low_findings.description: Python scripts perform file write operations for PDF output and JSON data. All file paths are provided as command-line arguments by the user, with no hardcoded paths or unauthorized file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File operations use user-provided paths from command-line arguments for legitimate PDF processing functionality

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Scripts contain hardcoded URLs pointing to PDF specification documentation (Adobe, WestHealth). These are reference links for developers, not network exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to public PDF specification documentation, used as reference comments in code

content.user_title: Manipulate PDF documents and fill forms programmatically
content.value_statement: PDF processing tasks require specialized tools for extraction, manipulation, and form filling. This skill provides comprehensive PDF handling capabilities using Python libraries and command-line tools.
actual_capabilities: Extract text and tables from PDF documents using pdfplumber and pypdf
Merge multiple PDFs into a single document or split PDFs into individual pages
Fill PDF form fields programmatically with validation and error checking
Create new PDFs from scratch using reportlab or pdf-lib
Convert PDF pages to images for OCR or visual analysis
Add annotations, watermarks, and rotate pages in PDF documents
limitations: Requires Python libraries (pypdf, pdfplumber, reportlab) to be installed in the environment
OCR functionality requires additional pytesseract and tesseract-ocr installation
Command-line tools (qpdf, pdftotext, pdfimages) need poppler-utils installed
Cannot process password-protected PDFs without the decryption password

use_cases.title: Extract data from PDF reports
use_cases.description: Automatically extract text content and tables from financial or scientific PDF reports for data analysis
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts processing multiple PDF documents

use_cases.title: Fill PDF application forms
use_cases.description: Programmatically complete fillable PDF forms with user-provided data and validation
use_cases.target_user: Administrative staff handling form processing workflows

use_cases.title: Batch PDF document processing
use_cases.description: Merge, split, rotate, and watermark multiple PDF documents in automated workflows

prompt_templates.title: Extract text from a PDF
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all text content from the attached PDF document and summarize the key information.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Basic text extraction from a single PDF

prompt_templates.title: Merge multiple PDFs
prompt_templates.prompt: Combine these PDF files into a single document in the order: cover.pdf, chapter1.pdf, chapter2.pdf, appendix.pdf

prompt_templates.title: Fill a PDF form with user data
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to fill out this application form. Extract the field information first, then I'll provide the values to populate each field.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Form field extraction and filling

prompt_templates.title: Extract tables and convert to Excel
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all tables from this financial report PDF and save them as an Excel spreadsheet with separate sheets for each table
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - Table extraction with structured output

output_examples.output: Successfully extracted 2,450 characters from 5 pages. Key sections identified: Executive Summary, Financial Data, Conclusions.
Created merged.pdf (15 pages total) combining: file1.pdf (3 pages), file2.pdf (7 pages), file3.pdf (5 pages)
Filled 12 form fields across 2 pages. Output saved to form_filled.pdf with validated field values.

best_practices: Always validate PDF form field values before filling to prevent errors
Use high-resolution settings (300+ DPI) when converting PDFs to images for OCR
Check bounding box intersections when adding annotations to non-fillable PDFs
anti_patterns: Do not skip visual validation of bounding boxes when filling non-fillable forms
Avoid processing password-protected PDFs without first decrypting them
Do not assume all PDFs have extractable text - scanned PDFs require OCR

faq.question: What Python libraries do I need installed?
faq.answer: Core libraries: pypdf, pdfplumber, reportlab. Optional: pytesseract for OCR, pdf2image for PDF-to-image conversion. Install with: pip install pypdf pdfplumber reportlab

faq.question: How do I handle scanned PDFs without text?
faq.answer: Scanned PDFs require OCR processing. Use the pytesseract workflow: convert PDF pages to images with pdf2image, then apply pytesseract.image_to_string() to extract text.

faq.question: Can this skill fill PDF forms that aren't fillable?
faq.answer: Yes, by adding text annotations at calculated positions. The process involves converting the PDF to images, identifying field locations visually, creating bounding boxes, and adding annotations.

faq.question: What command-line tools are available?
faq.answer: poppler-utils (pdftotext, pdfimages, pdftoppm), qpdf for merging/splitting, and pdftk for advanced operations. Install poppler-utils and qpdf via your system package manager.

faq.question: How do I extract tables from PDFs?
faq.answer: Use pdfplumber's extract_tables() method. For complex tables, configure table_settings with vertical_strategy and horizontal_strategy parameters for better detection.

faq.question: Can I process encrypted PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes, if you have the password. Use pypdf's decrypt() method or qpdf --password option. Without the password, encrypted PDFs cannot be processed.
